Atty. John Heller, Esq. of Rogers Joseph O'Donnell, 415.956.2828, for Defendant JMA;
No appearance by any other party or counsel.

Compliance Hearing On Final Approval Of Settlement.

The matter is called. NO ARGUMENT is held.

The matter is off calendar. The Court states that it is in receipt of the settlement administrator's declaration, that all but nine of the class members had no objection, and that that there was one opt out. The Court takes the matter off calendar and congratulates the parties on the settlement.

Notice Of Motion And Memorandum Of Points And Autho;Rities In Support Of Unopposed Motion For Final Approval Of Settlement; * Court Reporter Fees Paid*
Off Calendar Apr-01-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Jeffrey Cereghino (415-433-4949; jbc@cereghinolaw.com) of Cereghino Law Group for Plaintiffs.
James Holder (415-986-5900) of Gordon & Rees Scully Mansukhani LLP for Defendants Cocoa PRC II, et al.
John Heller (415-956-2828; jheller@rjo.com) of Rogers Joseph O'Donnell for Defendant JMA Ventures.

Hearing is held (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). Mr. Cereghino stated payments will be made within 45 days. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF STEPHEN SCHNEIDER's Motion For Final Approval Of Settlement is GRANTED.
Compliance hearing is set for May 18, 2021 at 9:30am in dept 302. Declaration from administrator to be filed a few days prior to that date.

Counsel for Plaintiff to submit proposed order with corrected dates.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
Off Calendar Apr-01-2021 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Plaintiff Class Application For Attorneys Fees, Costs And Service Award
Off Calendar Mar-23-2021 Continued To Apr-01-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
PLAINTIFF STEPHEN SCHNEIDER'S Notice Of Motion And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Plaintiff Class Application For Attorneys Fees, Costs And Service Award.

Appearances (via ZOOM Videoconference):
Atty. Jeffrey Cereghino, Esq. of Cereghino Law Group, 415.433.4949, for Plaintiff;
Atty. James Holder, Esq. of Gordon Rees, 415-986-92828 for Defendants Cocoa PRC II, et al.
Atty. John Heller, Esq. of Rogers Joseph O'Donnell, 415.956.2828, Defendant JMA Ventures.

Hearing On Final Approval/Fairness Hearing.

The Court's tentative ruling instructed the parties to appear.

The Court notes that there is no one present at today's hearing indicating an intent to object to the settlement.

DISCUSSIONS are held.

Mr. Cereghino notes that there were only four opt outs, states that the number of months / monthly calculations were incorrect, that it was corrected, that they sent emails out to 140 individuals, that he could speak to two of the four opt outs, and that if the two opt outs did so because of the number of months that he would let them back in.

The Court states that it will defer granting the Final Approval until Mr. Cereghino files a declaration addressing the opt out individuals. In addition, the settlement administrator is to provide a declaration addressing what happened, when it happened, and how it was fixed. The settlement administrator's declaration can also include attachments that can provide any information about the objections. Both declarations are to be on file by the close of business on March 30, 2021.

The Court continues the Hearing On Final Approval/Fairness Hearing to April 1, 2021 at 9:30 a.m. in Dept. 302. The Court states that no further notice is required since no class members are present.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com. (302/EPS)

MOTION / Notice Of Motion And Memorandum Of Points & Authorities In Support Of Unopposed Motion For Preliminary Approval
Off Calendar Dec-29-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. JEFFREY CEREGHINO, Esq. of Cereghino Law Group (Tel: 415-433-4949) for Plaintiff Stephen Schneider;
Atty. JOHN HELLER, Esq. of Rogers Joseph O'Donnell (Tel: 415-956-2828) for Defendant JMA Ventures.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF STEPHEN SCHNEIDER's Unopposed Motion For Preliminary Approval.

The Court's tentative ruling instructed the parties to appear.

The Court informs the parties that it is now in receipt of the amended class notice attached to plaintiff's supplemental declaration, but that the Court needs more time to review the document.

The Court is informed that Defendant JMA Ventures approves plaintiff's revised proposed order as to form. The Court instructs plaintiff to email that revised proposed order to the Court at contestdept302tr@sftc.org.

The Court indicates that if all the documentation in order, the Court intends to sign the revised proposed order today.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/EPS)

MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF STEPHEN SCHNEIDER's Unopposed Motion For Preliminary Approval is denied without prejudice.

[see word minute for details]

The supplemental brief, together with a revised proposed class notice and any amendments to the proposed class action settlement agreement, shall be filed by December 8, and the Court will hold a continued hearing on the motion for preliminary approval on December 17. If the parties need additional time, they may stipulate to an extended briefing and hearing schedule and contact the calendar clerk to reschedule.
